openmeter/watermill/eventbus.purpose: Wraps Watermill's cqrs.EventBus to route typed domain events to one of three Kafka topics (ingest, system, balance-worker) by event-name prefix matching against EventVersionSubsystem constants. The Publisher interface is the single injection point for all producers; topic routing is fully encapsulated.

patterns.name: Topic routing by event-name prefix
patterns.description: GeneratePublishTopic checks strings.HasPrefix against ingestevents.EventVersionSubsystem+'.' and balanceworkerevents.EventVersionSubsystem+'.'; everything else falls to SystemEventsTopic with no error. New event families must declare a matching EventVersionSubsystem constant.

patterns.name: PublishIfNoError for handler-inline publishing
patterns.description: Use p.WithContext(ctx).PublishIfNoError(handler(ctx, event)) to avoid a separate error check when a handler returns (marshaler.Event, error). Nil event signals no-publish.

patterns.name: Options.Validate before construction
patterns.description: Options and TopicMapping have Validate() methods called by New(); all three topic names, a non-nil Publisher and Logger are required.
patterns.example: if err := opts.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: NewMock for unit tests
patterns.description: Use eventbus.NewMock(t) — it wires a noop.Publisher with fixed topic names. Never instantiate a real Kafka-backed publisher in unit tests.

patterns.name: Nil-event guard suppresses publish
patterns.description: publisher.Publish silently ignores nil events; handlers that conditionally skip publishing return nil rather than an empty struct.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: TopicMapping, Options, Publisher/ContextPublisher interfaces, publisher struct, New(), NewMock().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a 4th topic requires updating TopicMapping, Validate(), and the GeneratePublishTopic switch; the new EventVersionSubsystem prefix must not overlap existing ones or routing silently misroutes.

anti_patterns: Publishing directly to a Kafka topic string instead of via eventbus.Publisher.
Defining event names without an EventVersionSubsystem constant — defaults to SystemEventsTopic silently.
Passing a nil Publisher in Options — New() errors; use noop.Publisher for tests.
Checking event == nil at call sites — the publisher already guards against nil events.
Creating a second Publisher instance outside app/common Wire wiring — TopicMapping must be consistent across producers.

decisions.decision: Three fixed topics, prefix-routed by EventVersionSubsystem.
decisions.rationale: Topic isolation matches worker topology (sink-worker, balance-worker, everything else); the event name carries the routing key, decoupling producers from consumer topology.

decisions.decision: Publisher interface instead of exposing cqrs.EventBus directly.
decisions.rationale: Hides Watermill internals, allows noop substitution in tests/DI, and provides WithContext/PublishIfNoError to avoid error-check boilerplate.

code_examples.scenario: Publishing a domain event using PublishIfNoError

openmeter/watermill/grouphandler.purpose: Provides NoPublishingHandler, a multiplexed Watermill consumer handler that dispatches a single Kafka message to all registered GroupEventHandlers matching the CloudEvents ce_type header, with per-type OTel metrics. Unknown event types are silently ACKed (dropped) to support rolling deploys.

patterns.name: NewGroupEventHandler generic constructor
patterns.description: Use NewGroupEventHandler[T](func(ctx, *T) error) to register a typed handler; T is the concrete event struct. Do not implement GroupEventHandler manually.
patterns.example: grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler(func(ctx context.Context, ev *billingevents.InvoiceCreated) error { return svc.OnInvoiceCreated(ctx, ev) })

patterns.name: Silent drop on unknown event type
patterns.description: If no handler matches ce_type, the message is ACKed and the ignored metric incremented; never return an error for unknown types so workers tolerate schema evolution.
patterns.example: if !ok || len(groupHandler) == 0 { h.meters.handlerMessageCount.Add(..., meterAttributeStatusIgnored); return nil }

patterns.name: errors.Join fan-out over all handlers
patterns.description: All handlers for the same event type receive the same deserialized event pointer via errors.Join(lo.Map(...)); any failure causes Watermill retry — no partial success.
patterns.example: err := errors.Join(lo.Map(groupHandler, func(h GroupEventHandler, _ int) error { return h.Handle(msg.Context(), event) })...)

patterns.name: AddHandler for post-construction registration
patterns.description: Use AddHandler to register additional handlers after NewNoPublishingHandler returns; it acquires the write lock safely.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: NoPublishingHandler mux, GroupEventHandler alias, NewGroupEventHandler generic, OTel meters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The first handler in the slice for a type is used for Unmarshal (NewEvent()); all handlers share one deserialized pointer — mutating it races across handlers.

anti_patterns: Returning an error for unrecognised event types — causes retry and DLQ of valid messages from other families on the same topic.
Mutating the event pointer inside a handler when multiple handlers share the same type — they share one deserialized instance.
Bypassing NoPublishingHandler with a raw message.NoPublishHandlerFunc — per-type OTel metrics and type-dispatch would be missing.
Passing a non-pointer event struct (with pointer receivers) to NewGroupEventHandler — NewEvent() deserialization fails.

decisions.decision: errors.Join fan-out rather than early-exit on handler failure.
decisions.rationale: All handlers for an event type are equal observers; partial failure must surface so Watermill retries rather than silently swallowing one handler's failure.

code_examples.scenario: Registering typed handlers and wiring into the router

openmeter/watermill/marshaler.purpose: Implements the cqrs.CommandEventMarshaler contract using CloudEvents 1.0 as the wire format. Each domain event serializes to a CloudEvents JSON envelope with ce_type/ce_time/ce_source/ce_subject headers; deserialization validates via Event.Validate(). WithSource injects the source field without modifying the event struct.

patterns.name: Event interface — EventName, EventMetadata, Validate
patterns.description: Each publishable struct implements EventName() (ce_type and dispatch key), EventMetadata() (source/subject/time/id), and Validate() (called on both Marshal and Unmarshal).

patterns.name: WithSource wrapper to inject CloudEvents source
patterns.description: Use marshaler.WithSource(sourceURI, ev) when the event struct lacks a source; MarshalJSON delegates to the inner event to avoid an extra 'Event' wrapper key.

patterns.name: ULID auto-ID and zero-time auto-now
patterns.description: NewCloudEvent auto-generates a ULID ID and sets time to now if EventMetadata returns empty values; events needing deterministic IDs set metadata.ID explicitly.

patterns.name: Validate called on both sides of the wire
patterns.description: Marshal calls Validate before encoding; Unmarshal calls it after JSON decode. Do not skip Validate even for optional-looking fields.
patterns.example: return ev.Validate() // last line of Unmarshal

patterns.name: UnknownEventName fallback for non-Event types
patterns.description: Name() returns 'io.openmeter.unknown' for types not implementing Event, which silently routes to SystemEventsTopic. All published structs must implement Event.

key_file_guides.role: Marshal (Event->CloudEvents JSON+headers), Unmarshal (->Event+Validate), Name, NameFromMessage, NewCloudEvent helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Name() returns UnknownEventName for non-Event values, silently routing to SystemEventsTopic; ensure all published structs implement Event.

key_file_guides.role: eventWithSource wrapper overriding EventMetadata().Source; MarshalJSON delegates to inner event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Pass pointer events to WithSource — value receivers on the inner Event may not satisfy the interface.

key_file_guides.role: WithSource round-trip test confirming source header and payload round-trip.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Real events must implement all three Event methods or Validate fails during Unmarshal.

anti_patterns: Implementing EventName() with an unstable string that changes between versions — routing and dispatch rely on stable names.
Returning a non-pointer from WithSource when the inner Event uses pointer receivers — MarshalJSON override needs pointer semantics.
Setting CloudEventsHeaderType manually in message metadata — Marshal sets it from EventName().
Skipping Validate() in event structs — Unmarshal calls it; invalid events trigger retries.
Embedding a non-Event type and passing it to Marshal — Name() returns UnknownEventName and routing falls to SystemEventsTopic.

decisions.rationale: Provides a standard envelope consumable by non-Go systems and inspectable in Kafka without custom schema knowledge, enabling cross-team debugging and polyglot consumers.

decisions.decision: Validate called on both producer and consumer sides.
decisions.rationale: Catches corrupt/partial events at publish and consume time, preventing invalid data from propagating and making schema violations visible at the source.

code_examples.scenario: Defining a new domain event satisfying marshaler.Event

openmeter/watermill/nopublisher.purpose: Provides two pure adapter functions converting between Watermill's NoPublishHandlerFunc and HandlerFunc signatures, used when a handler is logically a consumer but must fit a router slot expecting a different signature.

patterns.name: NoPublisherHandlerToHandlerFunc for consumer-only handlers
patterns.description: Wraps a NoPublishHandlerFunc into a HandlerFunc that always returns (nil, err); use when the router requires HandlerFunc but the handler produces no messages.

patterns.name: HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler with production guard
patterns.description: Converts a HandlerFunc into a NoPublishHandlerFunc; returns ErrMessagesProduced if the inner handler emits any messages. Only wrap truly side-effect-only handlers.

key_file_guides.role: Two pure adapter functions and one sentinel error (ErrMessagesProduced); no state.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrMessagesProduced is a hard error — a wrapped HandlerFunc returning messages causes infinite retries. Only wrap handlers guaranteed to return nil messages.

anti_patterns: Using HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler on a handler that conditionally produces messages — ErrMessagesProduced causes infinite retries.
Reimplementing these adapters inline in worker code instead of importing this package.

openmeter/watermill/router.purpose: Constructs the standard Watermill *message.Router with a fixed, ordered middleware stack: PoisonQueue (DLQ), DLQ telemetry with OTel tracing, CorrelationID, Recoverer, exponential-backoff Retry, optional ProcessingTimeout+RestoreContext, and per-handler HandlerMetrics. All worker binaries must use NewDefaultRouter.

patterns.name: Fixed middleware order via NewDefaultRouter
patterns.description: Middleware order: PoisonQueue -> DLQTelemetry -> CorrelationID -> Recoverer -> Retry -> (RestoreContext+Timeout if configured) -> HandlerMetrics. New workers call NewDefaultRouter, not their own router.

patterns.name: RestoreContext immediately before Timeout
patterns.description: Timeout middleware permanently replaces msg.Context(); RestoreContext saves and restores the original context so retries start fresh. Always pair them in this order.

patterns.name: MaxRetries off-by-one correction
patterns.description: Watermill's Retry runs MaxRetries+1 times; NewDefaultRouter subtracts 1. config.RetryConfiguration.MaxRetries is the true total attempt count; MaxRetries=0 means one attempt, no retry.

patterns.name: WarningLogSeverityError for transient errors
patterns.description: Wrap expected transient errors with router.NewWarningLogSeverityError; DLQTelemetry downgrades these to WARN but the message still goes to DLQ.

patterns.name: Options.Validate before construction
patterns.description: Options.Validate() checks Subscriber, Publisher, Logger, MetricMeter, Tracer, and Config.Validate(); missing MetricMeter/Tracer causes nil-pointer panics in HandlerMetrics.
patterns.example: if err := opts.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Real OTel providers required in tests
patterns.description: All OTel instruments are created at router construction; pass real sdkmetric/sdktrace providers in tests — nil values are rejected by Validate.

key_file_guides.role: NewDefaultRouter: builds *message.Router with the full middleware stack from Options; config from config.ConsumerConfiguration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DLQ.Topic and ConsumerGroupName must be set in Config; they are not validated by Options.Validate but missing values panic at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerMetrics (per-handler attempt metrics) and NewDLQTelemetryMiddleware (full lifecycle OTel span); both used internally.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewDLQTelemetryMiddleware wraps the whole retry+DLQ span in one OTel span — do not add another span wrapper or traces nest incorrectly.

key_file_guides.role: RestoreContext middleware: saves msg.Context() before the handler, restores after. Must precede Timeout.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Timeout without RestoreContext means retries share a cancelled context and fail immediately regardless of ProcessingTimeout.

key_file_guides.role: WarningLogSeverityError sentinel; DLQTelemetry checks via errors.As to downgrade log level.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only affects log severity — the message still goes to DLQ; not a way to suppress DLQ routing.

key_file_guides.role: warningOnlyLogger downgrading Error->Warn for retry noise, used only in the Retry middleware.
key_file_guides.watch_for: warningOnlyLogger.With() returns a new logger — use the returned adapter, not the original.

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven DLQ/retry/timeout tests using gochannel; reference for valid Options in tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use the DoneCondition done.Wait(120s) pattern, never time.Sleep.

anti_patterns: Constructing a *message.Router directly instead of NewDefaultRouter — middleware order, DLQ wiring, and OTel tracing will be absent.
Adding Timeout middleware without RestoreContext immediately before it — cancelled contexts break retries.
Setting MaxRetries=0 expecting 'no retries with no DLQ' — that means one attempt then DLQ; use MaxRetries=1 for one retry.
Returning non-WarningLogSeverityError for expected transient failures — they log at ERROR and may trigger alerts.
Skipping Options.Validate — missing Tracer/MetricMeter causes nil-pointer panics in HandlerMetrics.

decisions.rationale: PoisonQueue must catch all errors including Recoverer's; HandlerMetrics must measure only actual handler time, not retry/DLQ overhead.
Timeout permanently replaces msg.Context(); without RestoreContext the next retry starts with a cancelled context, failing immediately.

decisions.decision: warningOnlyLogger for the Retry middleware only.
decisions.rationale: Retry errors are expected transient failures; logging at ERROR would pollute alerts — only final DLQ disposition is an error log.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing the default router in a worker binary
